paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a portfolio
For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits.
calculatePositionImpacts
check Operation parameters
Integer identifier for a position
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.
calculatePositionImpactsOnly
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents investor management operations including creating, retrieving, encrypting, and removing investor records. Provides encryption key generation for protecting personally identifiable information (PII) and random identifier generation for privacy-compliant investor tracking in ESG Credits.

codeExamples.description: Generates an encryption key for encrypting and decrypting personally identifiable information
Creates an investor record with encrypted personally identifiable information including name and email

useCases: Register investors in green bond and ESG investment programs
Protect investor privacy through PII encryption in ESG Credits data repository
Generate random investor identifiers for GDPR-compliant tracking
Manage investor portfolios with secure personal information storage
Decrypt investor data for generating personalized ESG impact reports

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Call generateEncryptionKey operation to create a secure key for PII encryption and store it safely outside the ESG Credits system

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Identifier
implementationSteps.description: Use generateInvestorIdentifier to create random identifiers or provide custom identifiers that are not personally identifiable

implementationSteps.title: Add Investor
implementationSteps.description: Call addInvestor with investor_identifier, investor_name, investor_currency_code, investor_email, and encryption_key to register investor with encrypted PII

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Investor Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getInvestors with decryption_key parameter to view decrypted investor information and associated positions

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents issuer management operations for green asset issuers including creating, retrieving, and removing issuer records. Supports public and private visibility settings to control cross-account access, enabling management of banks, corporations, and institutions issuing green bonds or sustainable financial instruments.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all public green asset issuers available across different BWS accounts
Creates a new green asset issuer with EUR currency and private visibility

useCases: Register banks and financial institutions issuing green bonds
Track corporate issuers of sustainable finance instruments
Manage public issuers accessible across multiple ESG Credits accounts
Associate issuers with specific green assets and frameworks
Remove issuer records when no longer offering green financial products

implementationSteps.title: Create Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer operation with issuer_identifier, issuer_name, issuer_currency_code, and issuer_visibility (public or private)

implementationSteps.title: Link to Assets
implementationSteps.description: When creating green assets, reference the issuer_identifier to associate assets with their issuing institutions

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuers
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers with optional issuer_visibility parameter to view registered issuers and their details

Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides portfolio management operations for organizing investor green asset investments. Enables creation of portfolios, addition and removal of positions, and consolidated impact reporting across multiple investments. Supports investor-specific and asset-specific portfolio views with PII decryption capabilities.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all portfolios for a specific investor filtered by asset with decrypted personal information
Adds an investment position to an investor's green portfolio for consolidated impact tracking

useCases: Organize multiple green bond investments into investor portfolios
Generate consolidated ESG impact reports across portfolio holdings
Track asset allocation within sustainable investment portfolios
Manage separate portfolios for different investment strategies or time periods
Calculate total environmental benefits from diversified green asset portfolios

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio with investor_identifier and portfolio_identifier to establish a new portfolio for organizing investments

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio with portfolio_identifier and position_id to associate existing investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: View Portfolio Contents
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolioPositions with portfolio_identifier to retrieve all investments and their details within the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Portfolio Impacts
implementationSteps.description: Call getPortfolioImpacts to retrieve consolidated ESG benefits across all positions in the portfolio with optional filtering

Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents position management operations for tracking individual green asset investments by investors. Enables creation, retrieval, and removal of investment positions with automatic impact calculation, supporting multi-currency investments with term specifications and start dates for ESG certificate generation.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all investment positions for a specific investor filtered by asset and issuer with decrypted information
Creates a new investment position for 2.5M EUR over 180 days with automatic ESG impact calculation

useCases: Register investor purchases of green bonds and sustainable financial instruments
Track investment amounts, currencies, and terms for each green asset position
Automatically calculate ESG impacts when positions are created with reporting year specified
Manage position lifecycle from creation through impact calculation to removal
Generate ESG certificates using position start dates and calculated environmental benefits

implementationSteps.title: Create Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor_identifier, asset_identifier, position_amount, position_currency_code, and optional reporting_year for automatic impact calculation

implementationSteps.title: Specify Investment Term
implementationSteps.description: Include position_days (default 365) and position_start_date (YYYY-MM-DD format) to define the investment period for accurate impact allocation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions with investor_identifier and optional filters for asset_identifier, issuer_identifier, and user_identifier to view investments

implementationSteps.title: Remove Position
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition with investor_identifier and position_id to delete investment records including associated impact data

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents green project management and consolidation operations including creating projects, adding year-specific details, registering impact indicators, and building frameworks from multiple projects. Supports project-level ESG benefit tracking for renewable energy, energy efficiency, and circular economy initiatives with public/private visibility.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all public green projects for the 2023 reporting year
Consolidates multiple project impacts into a single green bond framework for the reporting year

useCases: Register wind park and solar farm projects with specific ESG benefits
Track allocated amounts and impact indicators for individual renewable energy projects
Consolidate multiple project benefits into comprehensive Green Bond Frameworks
Report emission reductions and energy production at project level
Manage public projects visible across accounts and private projects for internal use

implementationSteps.title: Create Project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject with project_identifier, project_name, project_currency_code, framework_identifier, and project_visibility

implementationSteps.title: Add Year Specifics
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject with allocated_amount and reporting_year to set funding details for the project period

implementationSteps.title: Register Impact Data
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject with sub_category_id, sub_category_volume, indicator_id, indicator_value, and reporting_year for each ESG benefit

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate to Framework
implementationSteps.description: Call buildFrameworkFromProjects with framework_identifier and reporting_year to aggregate project-level impacts into a framework

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy operations for building ESG classification systems including categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Provides access to UN Sustainable Development Goals and EU Environmental Objectives for mapping green activities to internationally recognized sustainability frameworks with multi-language support.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals available for taxonomy mapping

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies aligned with organizational sustainability goals
Map green activities to UN Sustainable Development Goals for standardized reporting
Associate environmental projects with EU Environmental Objectives for regulatory compliance
Define impact categories like renewable energy, energy efficiency, and circular economy
Create multi-language taxonomy structures for international ESG reporting

implementationSteps.title: Review Standard Goals
implementationSteps.description: Call getSDGs and getEUObjectives to explore available UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives for taxonomy alignment

implementationSteps.title: Build Category Structure
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory and addSubCategory operations with category_name, reporting_year, and language_code to create taxonomy hierarchy

implementationSteps.title: Define Indicators and Units
implementationSteps.description: Create super indicators, indicators, and units using corresponding add operations to specify measurable ESG benefits

implementationSteps.title: Link Goals to Categories
implementationSteps.description: Call addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory with sub_category_id, sdg_id, and eu_objective_id to map taxonomy elements to international frameworks

pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing multilingual translations in ESG Credits taxonomy. Covers operations to add language translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units across different reporting years. Supports 12 languages including English, Spanish, German, and French.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all available languages for ESG taxonomy translations
Adds or changes a language translation for a taxonomy category
Adds language translation for an ESG indicator for a specific reporting year

useCases: Create multilingual ESG reporting frameworks
Localize impact indicators for international investors
Translate environmental taxonomy for different markets
Support multi-language investor reporting across regions
Enable cross-border ESG compliance documentation

implementationSteps.title: Get Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to retrieve all supported language codes (en, sv, dk, nb, fi, de, pl, fr, nl, es, pt, it) for taxonomy translations

implementationSteps.title: Add Category Translations
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation, and addIndicatorTranslation operations to register translations for each taxonomy element and reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Translate Units
implementationSteps.description: Call addUnitTranslation for each measurement unit, even if identical across languages, as this entry is required by the model

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing end users, groups, and investor access control in ESG Credits. Provides operations to create, update, and remove users, assign them to functional groups (bankers, investors, capitalists), and control access to specific investor portfolios. Supports user encryption for data security.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user or updates existing user information with encryption support
Assigns a user to a functional group to control access to specific functionality
Grants a user access to view specific investor portfolio information

useCases: Manage user access to ESG investment portfolios
Assign banking professionals to investor groups
Control regulatory access to ESG compliance data
Create role-based permissions for ESG reporting platforms
Secure investor data with user-level encryption

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation to register end users with identifiers, names, email, role (user or admin), and optional encryption keys

implementationSteps.title: Assign Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call addGroupToUser to connect users to predefined groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators) for access control

implementationSteps.title: Grant Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser operation to give specific users permission to access individual investor portfolios and ESG impact data

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the ESG Credits solution for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels and investment platforms. Explains use cases for banks, institutional investors, venture capitalists, and issuers. Features include ICMA framework support, custom taxonomy, SDG mapping, blockchain certification, and multi-currency calculations.
useCases: Banks providing investor reports on green asset portfolios to retail customers
Institutional investors tracking ESG impact of green asset investments
Venture capitalists reporting consolidated ESG benefits to project sponsors
Asset issuers publishing impact details for promotional and calculation purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured green project characteristics

implementationSteps.title: Define Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Set up your ESG taxonomy with categories, indicators, and units. Support ICMA framework or custom taxonomy with two levels of categories and indicators

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Frameworks
implementationSteps.description: Create impact profiles for green frameworks tied to UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives, supporting multiple currencies and languages

implementationSteps.title: Calculate and Report
implementationSteps.description: Generate impact reports per annum or investment term, consolidate project benefits, and optionally store ESG profiles immutably on blockchain

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. Previous ESG Credits Next ESG Credits API Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful?

paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Calendar objects representing scheduled games, matches, or tournaments. Calendars are time-bound events associated with specific Fields, enabling season-based or single-event fan campaigns. Includes operations to create, list, and delete calendars with unique IDs for linking game objects.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for a season or tournament (e.g., LaLiga 2025-26)
Lists all calendars or retrieves a specific calendar by ID
Deletes a calendar by its unique calendar ID

useCases: Create UEFA Champions League Final calendar for single events
Schedule entire LaLiga season with multiple matches
Manage tournament timelines for fan engagement campaigns
Link scheduled matches to specific sports fields
Organize multi-sport events across different time periods

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with a name parameter (e.g., '2025-26') to create a scheduled event or season. Returns a calendarId for linking matches

implementationSteps.title: List Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to retrieve all created calendars or a specific calendar by providing the calendarId parameter

implementationSteps.title: Delete When Complete
implementationSteps.description: Remove completed calendars using delete_calendar operation by providing the calendarId

pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Cubes, which are NFT zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is uniquely owned by fans, tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Includes operations to list cubes with pagination and update cube prices in USD cents.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a field with pagination support, returning cube IDs and coordinate data
Updates the price of multiple cubes in USD cents (e.g., 500 = $5.00)

useCases: Fans purchasing NFT cubes in virtual sports fields
Track cube ownership during live match events
Distribute rewards to cube owners based on game actions
Set dynamic pricing for premium field zones
Query cube coordinates for event impact calculations

implementationSteps.title: List Field Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Call list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters to retrieve cubes. Use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination through hundreds of cubes

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Use update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (using i_j index format), and priceInCents to set cube prices for fan purchases

implementationSteps.title: Track Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Monitor cube status and ownership data returned in API responses to manage fan NFT holdings and reward distributions

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Event Types, which define in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or passes. Events are registered with spatial coordinates and timestamps, affecting cube owners whose zones match the action location. Each event type has configurable point values for rewarding fans.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type (e.g., 'pass') with assigned points (100) for a field
Lists all event types configured for a specific field
Updates the point value assigned to an event type (e.g., changing from 100 to 1000)

useCases: Define scoring rules for goals, assists, and passes
Assign point values to different in-game events
Reward cube owners when events occur in their zones
Track spatial game actions with coordinates and timestamps
Configure event types for different sports (soccer, basketball, chess)

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type to define game events (pass, goal, assist) with eventTypeName and eventTypePoints parameters. Returns eventTypeId for registration

implementationSteps.title: Configure Points
implementationSteps.description: Call update_event-type to adjust point values for events as needed, using eventTypeId and new eventTypePoints values

implementationSteps.title: Register Live Events
implementationSteps.description: When events occur during matches, use the eventTypeId to register events at specific coordinates, triggering rewards for matching cube owners

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Fields, which represent virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive cube zones. Fields are the foundational canvas for all game interactions, typically linked to real-world sports venues. Includes operations to create fields with customizable dimensions and cube sizes, list existing fields, and delete fields with all associated cubes.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new virtual field (Camp Nou) with dimensions in centimeters and 3x3 meter cubes
Lists all created fields or retrieves details for a specific field by ID
Deletes a field and all associated cubes automatically

useCases: Create virtual Real Madrid Stadium for match-based fan engagement
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into purchasable NFT zones
Configure field dimensions for different sports (football, basketball, cricket)
Generate cube grids automatically based on field and cube sizes
Link virtual fields to real-world sports venues for fan campaigns

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field with name, sport, fieldLength, fieldWidth (in centimeters), and cubeLength parameters. Cubes are automatically generated upon creation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Field Details
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to view all created fields or get specific field information by providing fieldId parameter

implementationSteps.title: Clean Up
implementationSteps.description: Delete fields with delete_field operation using fieldId. All associated cubes are automatically removed with the field

pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for registering Live Events during matches. Live Events capture in-game occurrences (goals, moves, fouls) at specific coordinates on a Field during scheduled Calendar matches. Can be recorded manually via dashboard, automatically via ML models, or through custom applications to power fan engagement and reward processing.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event at specific coordinates during a match for scoring and rewards

useCases: Record goals at specific field coordinates during live matches
Capture chess moves on tokenized board positions
Trigger automatic rewards for cube owners when events occur in their zones
Integrate AI-powered event detection for real-time game tracking
Manually register events via organizer dashboard during matches

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the in-game occurrence you want to register

implementationSteps.title: Call Event API
implementationSteps.description: Submit new_event API call with all required parameters to register the event at specific field coordinates during the scheduled match

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically maps event coordinates to affected cubes and processes reward points for cube owners based on event type points

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Matches in the FAN Game Cube system. A Match represents a time-bound gameplay session tied to a specific Field where events are tracked, points awarded, and fan interactions occur. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting matches with team information and scheduling.

codeExamples.description: Create a new match with team information, start time, and base64 encoded images that are automatically saved to IPFS
List matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by matchId to retrieve detailed match information
Response showing match details with IPFS URL for uploaded images

useCases: Schedule PSG vs Manchester City football match with team flags and details
Create Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears NFL game session
Set up Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura chess match
Track match status through scheduled, playing, finished, or canceled states
Upload match announcement images to IPFS automatically

implementationSteps.title: Create New Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, description, team names, team flags (base64), and optional price in USD cents

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match with matchId and calendarId to modify only the fields that need updating (name, description, status, etc.)

implementationSteps.title: List and Monitor Matches
implementationSteps.description: Retrieve match information using list_matches with calendarId, optionally filtering by matchId for specific match details

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships (Plays) in NFT Game Cube. This models the temporal lifecycle of a Field by linking static spatial configurations to dynamic calendar entries representing individual matches, tournaments, or rounds. Images are automatically saved to IPFS.

codeExamples.description: Create a new field-calendar relationship linking a sports field to a calendar with season description and image
List all calendars linked to a specific field to view tournaments and seasons
Response showing field-calendar relationships with IPFS URLs for uploaded images

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 tournament to a football field
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season on a basketball court
Associate multiple tournament calendars with a single sports ground
Track temporal lifecycle of field usage across different seasons
Store season branding images on IPFS for permanent access

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g., 'PSG 2025-26'), and optional base64 encoded image

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendars and tournaments associated with a specific field

implementationSteps.title: Delete Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Remove field-calendar link using delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId parameters

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prizes in NFT Game Cube matches. Prizes represent reward assets that users receive based on CUBE performance, including digital NFTs, token bonuses, physical goods, or experiences. Supports first, second, and third place prize positions with rich HTML descriptions and multiple images.

codeExamples.description: Create a new match prize with position (1-3), name, description, and snapshot image for first place winner
List all prizes configured for a specific match including IPFS image URLs
Update existing prize with new HTML description or other modified attributes

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top CUBE performers in a football match
Offer VIP match tickets as second place prize
Provide Zoom call or AMA with athlete for third place
Distribute bonus $BWS tokens to winning cube owners
Create physical goods rewards like signed frames from Phil Foden

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1, 2, or 3), prize name, description, and optional snapshot/images (base64 encoded)

implementationSteps.title: Update Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match-prize with prizeId to modify name, description, htmlDescription, or add additional images (image1, image2, image3)

implementationSteps.title: List and Delete Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Retrieve prizes using list_match-prizes with matchId, or remove prizes using delete_match-prize with prizeId

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of the FAN Game Cube platform for building revenue streams in sports. Enables fans to own NFT-based digital assets (cubes) linked to sports field sections, earning points from real-time events for rewards. Supports multiple sports including football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, and more.
useCases: Football stadiums divided into 500-2000 cubes for fans to purchase field sections
Chess tournaments tokenizing 64 chessboard squares for fan interaction
Tennis courts tokenizing baseline, service boxes, and net areas
Cricket fields dividing pitch into batting zones and boundary sections
Basketball courts including three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone as purchasable cubes

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine how many cubes to sell based on sport type (e.g., 500-2000 for football, 64 for chess) and divide playing area strategically

implementationSteps.title: Set Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Establish dynamic pricing with premium rates for high-demand sections (center field, penalty spots) and lower rates ($10+) for secondary zones

implementationSteps.title: Integrate Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Add one-click JavaScript purchase widget to website for seamless credit card transactions, enabling fans to buy cubes easily

implementationSteps.title: Launch Event and Track Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Publish event, let fans purchase cubes, track real-time events through mobile app with leaderboards, and distribute prizes to top performers

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 8 days ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Individual KOLs showcase competencies with verifiable performance metrics
KOL teams track performance accurately and show clients measurable results

pages.title: X Bot Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete command reference for X Bot covering account tracking, keyword/hashtag monitoring, cashtag tracking, mentions, exclusions, ignore lists, named filters, scheduling, and report generation. Commands are organized by category with usage examples, parameters, and admin permission requirements for Telegram group management.

codeExamples.description: Add X accounts to track, optionally assigning to a named filter for separate tracking configurations
Add keywords or hashtags to track, using quotes for multi-word phrases and hashtag symbols
Schedule automated daily reports at specific UTC time, optionally for a named filter

useCases: Track multiple X accounts and generate daily performance leaderboards
Monitor specific keywords and hashtags for community discussions
Track cryptocurrency cashtags like $BTC and $ETH
Filter out spam and scams using exclusion keywords
Create separate tracking configurations for different clients using named filters

implementationSteps.title: Add Tracking Targets
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts, /add_keywords, /add_cashtags, and /add_mentions to define what X content to track

implementationSteps.title: Configure Filters and Exclusions
implementationSteps.description: Set up named filters for separate tracking scenarios and add exclusions to filter out irrelevant content

implementationSteps.title: Schedule and Generate Reports
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule for automated daily reports or /recreate to generate immediate analytics with leaderboards

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Complete reference guide for all X Bot commands. Commands are organized by category for easy navigation. Basic Commands /help Display all available bot commands with brief descriptions. Usage: /help Parameters: None Response: Complete list of commands organized by category. Example: Copy /help X API Filtering Setup X Bot's filtering system allows you to define exactly what X (Twitter) content to track and analyze. You can track specific accounts, monitor keywords and hashtags, follow cryptocurrency cashtags, see who's mentioning important accounts, and filter out noise with exclusions. The filtering system is highly flexible and supports complex setups through named filters , which allow you to create multiple independent tracking configurations. This is particularly useful for KOL teams managing multiple clients, projects running different campaigns, or anyone who needs to separate different tracking scenarios. Each named filter can have its own set of accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and even independent report schedules. Account Management Track X (Twitter) accounts to monitor their posts and engagement metrics. /add_accounts Add one or more X accounts to track. Usage: /add_accounts @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X (Twitter) handles to track (with @ symbol) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Tracks all posts from specified accounts Monitors engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes accounts in leaderboard rankings /remove_accounts Remove one or more X accounts from tracking. Usage: /remove_accounts @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_accounts View all currently tracked X accounts. Usage: /get_accounts [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show accounts for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked X accounts, or accounts in the specified filter. Keyword Management Track specific keywords and hashtags to monitor community discussions. /add_keywords Add keywords or hashtags to track. Usage: /add_keywords "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to track (use quotes for multi-word phrases) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Hashtags should include the # symbol. /remove_keywords Remove keywords or hashtags from tracking. Usage: /remove_keywords "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_keywords View all currently tracked keywords and hashtags. Usage: /get_keywords [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show keywords for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked keywords and hashtags, or keywords in the specified filter. Cashtag Management Track cryptocurrency cashtags (e.g., $BTC, $ETH) to monitor token discussions. /add_cashtags Add cashtags to track. Usage: /add_cashtags $TAG1 $TAG2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: $TAG1 $TAG2 ... : Cashtags to track (with $ symbol) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Cashtags must include the $ symbol. /remove_cashtags Remove cashtags from tracking. Usage: /remove_cashtags $TAG1 $TAG2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: $TAG1 $TAG2 ... : Cashtags to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_cashtags View all currently tracked cashtags. Usage: /get_cashtags [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show cashtags for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked cashtags, or cashtags in the specified filter. Mention Management Track X account mentions to see who's talking about specific accounts. /add_mentions Add X accounts to track mentions of. Usage: /add_mentions @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to track mentions of filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Tracks posts that mention the specified accounts Shows who is talking about these accounts Includes mention frequency in reports /remove_mentions Remove X accounts from mention tracking. Usage: /remove_mentions @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop tracking mentions of filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_mentions View all accounts currently tracked for mentions. Usage: /get_mentions [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show mentions for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all accounts being tracked for mentions, or mentions in the specified filter. Exclusion Management Exclude specific keywords or hashtags from reports to filter out noise. /add_exclusions Add keywords or hashtags to exclude from tracking. Usage: /add_exclusions "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to exclude filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Excludes posts containing these keywords from reports Helps filter out spam, scams, or irrelevant content Improves report quality /remove_exclusions Remove keywords or hashtags from exclusion list. Usage: /remove_exclusions "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to stop excluding filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_exclusions View all currently excluded keywords and hashtags. Usage: /get_exclusions [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show exclusions for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all excluded keywords and hashtags, or exclusions in the specified filter. Ignore List Management Ignore specific X accounts so they don't appear in leaderboards (useful for hiding bots, test accounts, or team admins). /add_ignore_list Add X accounts to the ignore list. Usage: /add_ignore_list @account1 @account2 ... Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to ignore in leaderboards Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Hides specified accounts from leaderboard rankings Posts are still tracked but not shown in public reports Useful for internal/test accounts /remove_ignore_list Remove X accounts from the ignore list. Usage: /remove_ignore_list @account1 @account2 ... Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop ignoring Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_ignore_list View all accounts currently on the ignore list. Usage: /get_ignore_list Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: List of all ignored X accounts. Named Filters Named filters allow you to create multiple tracking configurations for different purposes (e.g., different clients, campaigns, or projects). Understanding Named Filters Named filters let you: Track different sets of accounts/keywords for different purposes Generate separate reports for different clients or campaigns Keep configurations organized and separate How to use named filters: 1 Create a named filter Add items with the filter=name parameter, for example: 2 Generate reports for a named filter Use the recreate command with the filter parameter: 3 View filter configuration Inspect items in a filter: Examples of Named Filter Usage Example 1: Multiple Client Tracking (KOL Team) Example 2: Different Campaign Tracking (Project) Schedule Management Automate report generation with scheduled daily reports. /set_schedule Schedule automated daily reports. Usage: /set_schedule HH:MM [filter=name] Parameters: HH:MM : Time in 24-hour format (UTC timezone) filter=name (optional): Schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Generates automated daily reports at the specified time Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to the group Time is in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) Timezone Conversion Examples: New York (EST): 12:00 UTC = 07:00 EST London (GMT): 12:00 UTC = 12:00 GMT Singapore (SGT): 12:00 UTC = 20:00 SGT /get_schedule View current report schedule. Usage: /get_schedule [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: Current scheduled report time in UTC, or message if no schedule is set. /delete_schedule Cancel automated daily reports. Usage: /delete_schedule [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Delete schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Removes scheduled report generation Reports can still be generated manually with /recreate Report Generation Generate analytics reports manually. /recreate Generate a new analytics report immediately. Usage: /recreate [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Generate report for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes (for manual generation) Examples: What this does: Creates performance report with current data Generates leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Posts performance screenshot to the group Shows engagement metrics and rankings Note: Report generation can take 1-2 minutes depending on data volume. /report Alternative command to generate analytics report. Usage: /report [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Generate report for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Functions identically to /recreate . Customization Customize report appearance and scoring to match your brand. /set_points Customize engagement point weights for performance scoring. Usage: /set_points <likes> <retweets> <replies> <quotes> <bookmarks> <impressions> Parameters: <likes> : Point weight for likes (default: 1) <retweets> : Point weight for retweets (default: 2) <replies> : Point weight for replies (default: 1.5) <quotes> : Point weight for quote tweets (default: 3) <bookmarks> : Point weight for bookmarks (default: 2) <impressions> : Point weight for impressions (default: 0.001) Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Customizes how performance scores are calculated Higher weights give more importance to that engagement type Affects leaderboard rankings Default Formula: /get_points View current engagement point weights. Usage: /get_points Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current point weights for all engagement types. /set_colors Customize colors used in performance reports. Usage: /set_colors <likes> <retweets> <replies> <quotes> <bookmarks> <impressions> Parameters: <likes> : Hex color code for likes (default: #FF6B6B) <retweets> : Hex color code for retweets (default: #4ECDC4) <replies> : Hex color code for replies (default: #45B7D1) <quotes> : Hex color code for quotes (default: #FFA07A) <bookmarks> : Hex color code for bookmarks (default: #98D8C8) <impressions> : Hex color code for impressions (default: #F7DC6F) Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Customizes visual appearance of reports Applies to performance screenshots Helps match your brand colors Note: Colors must be valid hex codes (e.g., #FF6B6B). /get_colors View current color configuration. Usage: /get_colors Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current hex color codes for all engagement types. /set_top_title Customize the title for the main leaderboard section. Usage: /set_top_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for main leaderboard section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Replaces default "Top Accounts by Performance" title Adds custom branding to reports Title appears in performance screenshots /set_best_title Customize the title for the best tweet section. Usage: /set_best_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for best tweet section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: /set_engagement_title Customize the title for the engagement scoring section. Usage: /set_engagement_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for engagement scoring section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: Project Settings Configure project metadata to enhance your presence on xbot.ninja. /set_project_name Set your project or team name. Usage: /set_project_name "Project Name" Parameters: "Project Name" : Name of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets project name displayed on xbot.ninja Appears in public leaderboards if you meet listing criteria Helps projects and KOLs get discovered /get_project_name View current project name. Usage: /get_project_name Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_description Set your project or team description. Usage: /set_project_description "Description text" Parameters: "Description text" : Brief description of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets description shown on xbot.ninja Helps users understand what your project/team does Appears in featured projects list /get_project_description View current project description. Usage: /get_project_description Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_logo Set your project or team logo URL. Usage: /set_project_logo "https://url-to-logo.png" Parameters: "https://url-to-logo.png" : Direct URL to your logo image Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets logo displayed on xbot.ninja Provides visual branding for your project/team Logo appears in featured projects showcase Note: Logo should be square format (1:1 ratio) and accessible via HTTPS. /get_project_logo View current project logo URL. Usage: /get_project_logo Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_long_description Set detailed project or team description. Usage: /set_project_long_description "Long description text" Parameters: "Long description text" : Detailed description of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets extended description for project detail pages Provides more context about your project/team Helps users make informed decisions /get_project_long_description View current long project description. Usage: /get_project_long_description Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_urls Set project or team URLs (website, social media, etc.). Usage: /set_project_urls "url1" "url2" "url3" ... Parameters: "url1" "url2" ... : URLs for website, social media, documentation, etc. Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets links displayed on xbot.ninja Provides ways for users to learn more about your project/team Helps drive traffic to your platforms /get_project_urls View current project URLs. Usage: /get_project_urls Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Getting Credits X Bot includes 100 FREE credits to get you started. Each credit corresponds to one post tracked in your filtering setup (accounts, keywords, cashtags, and mentions you've configured). Once you've used your free credits, you can purchase additional credits to continue tracking. /buy Purchase X Bot Pro Edition credits using BWS tokens. Usage: /buy Parameters: None Where: Works in both group chats and Direct Messages (DM) with the bot 🔒 PRIVACY RECOMMENDED: Use Direct Message (DM) for Private Purchases For complete privacy, send /buy in a Direct Message to @BWS_X_Bot instead of in the group chat. This keeps the entire purchase conversation private - only you will see the purchase link and transaction details. Examples: Recommended: In Direct Message with bot (completely private) In group chat (visible to everyone in the group) What this does: Provides secure purchase link for BWS token credit purchase Redirects to purchase page at xbot.ninja Credits are added to your group immediately after successful purchase How to purchase privately in DM: 1 Open a Direct Message with the bot Open a Direct Message with @BWS_X_Bot . 2 Send the buy command Send /buy command in the DM (not in the group). 3 Receive private link Bot sends you a private purchase link that only you can see. 4 Complete purchase Complete your purchase privately - no one in the group will see any part of the transaction. 5 Credits applied Credits are automatically added to your group upon successful payment. /subscription View your current Pro subscription status. Usage: /subscription Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current subscription status, expiration date, and available credits. /get_credits View available Pro credits for your group. Usage: /get_credits Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Number of remaining Pro credits for report generation and other Pro features. Command Tips & Best Practices General Tips Use Quotes for Multi-Word Terms: Always use quotes when adding keywords with spaces: Include Symbols: Don't forget @ for accounts, # for hashtags, $ for cashtags: Named Filters for Organization: Use named filters to keep different tracking scenarios organized: Schedule in UTC: Remember that schedule times are in UTC timezone. Convert from your local time. Performance Optimization Start Small: Begin with a few accounts/keywords and expand based on results Use Exclusions: Filter out spam and irrelevant content with exclusions Regular Reports: Set daily schedules to track trends over time Named Filters: Separate different campaigns or clients for clearer insights Report Quality Balanced Tracking: Track both accounts and keywords for comprehensive insights Clean Data: Use ignore list to hide test accounts and bots Custom Scoring: Adjust point weights to reflect what matters most to your community Branding: Set custom titles and colors to match your brand Privacy and Security DM for Privacy: Use Direct Messages for sensitive commands like /buy Admin Commands: Only admins can configure tracking and settings Public Reports: Anyone in the group can view reports and current configuration Filter Isolation: Named filters keep different client/campaign data separate Troubleshooting 1 No Data in Reports Verify accounts/keywords are configured: /get_accounts , /get_keywords Wait a few hours for X API to collect data Ensure tracked accounts are public 2 Reports Not Generating Check schedule is set: /get_schedule Verify time is in UTC Manually test with /recreate 3 Bot Not Responding Verify bot has admin status in the group Check if bot was removed or banned Try removing and re-adding the bot Advanced Usage Multi-Client KOL Team Setup: Comprehensive Project Tracking: This command reference is for X Bot Beta Version. Commands and features are subject to change as the platform improves. Previous X Bot Installation Next Getting Support Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? pages.summary: Installation guide for setting up X Bot in Telegram groups with three distinct paths: Crypto Project Setup for tracking project engagement, Individual KOL Setup for personal performance metrics, and KOL Team Setup for multi-member tracking. Includes step-by-step instructions, configuration examples, and troubleshooting.
