first analytics report 4 Review the output Ensure accounts and keywords are tracked correctly 5 Check scheduled reports Wait for your scheduled time to verify automation Common Issues and Solutions Bot Not Responding Possible causes: Bot not made admin in the group Bot was removed or banned Network connectivity issues Solutions: Verify bot has admin status Remove and re-add the bot Check Telegram server status No Data in Reports Possible causes: Accounts or keywords not configured Tracking period too short (wait a few hours) X accounts have privacy restrictions Solutions: Run /get_accounts and /get_keywords to verify configuration Wait for X API to collect data (can take a few hours) Ensure tracked X accounts are public Reports Not Generating Possible causes: No schedule set Schedule time in wrong timezone (should be UTC) Insufficient data for report generation Solutions: Run /get_schedule to verify configuration Set schedule with /set_schedule HH:MM (UTC time) Manually generate report with /recreate to test Installation takes just a few minutes. pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Overview What is X Bot? X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. Key Features Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post Example Calculation: For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions Customization & Branding Custom point weights for different engagement types Branded colors for visual reports Custom titles for leaderboard sections Project metadata (name, logo, description, URLs) Named filters for different tracking scenarios Automated Reporting Schedule daily reports at specific times (UTC) Automatic performance screenshots Leaderboards showing top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Historical tracking and trend analysis Dual-Purpose Analytics Platform X Bot serves multiple distinct but complementary audiences: For Project Owners Get a trusted measure of community engagement and hired KOLs' real profitability Measure your community's authentic X engagement and impact Track the real profitability and ROI of hired KOL campaigns Discover KOL teams with proven performance metrics Hire influencers who deliver actual engagement and results Monitor KOL performance with transparent, verifiable data Avoid influencers with fake followers or inflated metrics What you get: Trusted measurement of community engagement levels Real performance data on KOL teams and their ROI Comparative metrics across multiple influencers Evidence of authentic community building Data-driven decisions for influencer partnerships For KOLs and KOL Teams Showcase your competencies and get discovered Build a public portfolio of verifiable performance metrics Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Track team performance accurately to show clients measurable results Stand out from competitors with transparent data Demonstrate real ROI to potential clients For Investors Discover crypto projects with authentic community traction Visit xbot.ninja to explore our new investment discovery feature: Find projects with genuine X activity and real engagement Identify legitimate investment opportunities backed by actual community activity Analyze projects based on verifiable metrics, not vanity numbers Make data-driven investment decisions What to look for: Consistent engagement across multiple metrics Growing community participation Authentic conversations and discussions Real influencer involvement How Projects and KOLs Get Featured X Bot curates high-quality data from projects and KOL teams through a selection process. 1 X Bot Installation Must have successfully installed and configured X Bot in their Telegram community. Demonstrates commitment to transparent analytics. 2 Minimum Performance Threshold Achieve a minimum performance score indicating sustained X activity, showing genuine engagement over time. 3 Active Community Engagement Consistent posting and meaningful interactions, authentic community growth patterns, and regular engagement from community members. 4 Quality Content Standards High-quality X presence with genuine engagement metrics, no artificial inflation or bot-driven activity, and authentic interactions and conversations. Why Curation Matters The curation process ensures quality for all stakeholders: Investors: Find legitimate projects with real community backing Project Owners: Discover KOL teams that deliver actual engagement and ROI KOLs: Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Everyone: Access transparent, verifiable metrics reflecting authentic activity All projects and KOL teams are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. Use Cases Crypto Projects Boost community X engagement with competitions and prizes Track your project's official X account Monitor community hashtags and cashtags Run engagement competitions with transparent leaderboards Reward top performers with prizes Build authentic community participation Individual KOLs Get publicly listed to showcase your competencies Create a personal analytics tracking group Monitor your X account performance Build a verifiable portfolio of engagement metrics Get discovered by projects seeking influencers Prove your value with transparent data KOL Teams Track team performance and show clients real results Set up shared analytics for your entire team Track all team members' X accounts in one place Monitor project hashtags you're promoting Generate team performance reports Demonstrate ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Benefits Summary Transparency All metrics are verifiable and sourced directly from X (Twitter) No manipulation or artificial inflation Real-time data updates Authenticity Performance scoring weighs meaningful engagement over vanity metrics Curated selection ensures quality participants Continuous monitoring maintains standards Actionable Insights Daily automated reports with actionable data Leaderboards identifying top performers Historical trends and performance tracking Customizable views for different analysis needs Community Building Gamification through leaderboards and competitions Recognition for top contributors Customizable views for different analysis needs Previous X Bot Next X Bot Installation Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? Copy Performance Score = (Likes × 1) + (Retweets × 2) + (Replies × 1.5) + (Quotes × 3) + (Bookmarks × 2) + (Impressions × 0.001) Copy Performance Score = (50 × 1) + (10 × 2) + (8 × 1.5) + (3 × 3) + (15 × 2) + (5,000 × 0.001) = 50 + 20 + 12 + 9 + 30 + 5 = 126 points

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees Fees Calculator API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start Next API How Tos Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? Was this helpful? pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). Learn how to Get your API Key . Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save a message to the blockchain. Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error).

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. Available Networks We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "content": { "text": "Hello World!" } } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": [ { "": "" } ] } Copy curl --location 'https://api.staging.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "list" "parameters": { "from": 1700404140000 "to": 1700411940000 } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": [ { "timestamp": "1700416540067", "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "fileType": "json", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } }, { "timestamp": "1700121600009", "cid": "QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "description": "image for token 1325", "fileType": "image", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" } } ] }

pages.summary: Comprehensive guide to BWS IPFS service leveraging InterPlanetary File System for decentralized storage. Explains IPFS concepts, BWS gateway usage, key features like content addressing and decentralization, and supported file types including images, PDFs, and JSON.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and asset images on IPFS for blockchain applications
Host decentralized content delivery for static web resources
Share structured JSON data in transparent, verifiable way
Upload logos and graphics for decentralized app projects
Ensure data integrity and provenance through content addressing

implementationSteps.title: Access BWS IPFS Gateway
implementationSteps.description: Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to bridge IPFS network with standard HTTP protocols for browser access

implementationSteps.title: Upload supported file types
implementationSteps.description: Upload images, PDF, or JSON files to IPFS network using BWS API service

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve using CID
implementationSteps.description: Access uploaded content using the unique Content Identifier (CID) through IPFS URI or gateway URL

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. Previous BWS.IPFS.Upload Next Operations Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? BWS IPFS Gateway Key Features of IPFS Supported File Types Was this helpful?

pages.title: BWS.NFT.zK | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.NFT.zK enables NFT management with zero complexities, simplifying the creation and management of non-fungible tokens on blockchain networks. This solution abstracts the technical challenges of NFT operations including attribute and trait management.
useCases: Create NFT collections without blockchain coding knowledge
Manage NFT attributes and traits for token metadata
Deploy NFT smart contracts with simplified API calls
Build NFT marketplaces with easy token management
Generate NFTs for digital art and collectibles

pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains NFT attributes (traits) and their structure in NFT metadata. Covers how trait_type and value pairs define unique characteristics like fur color, eye type, and accessories. Emphasizes the importance of validating trait display in user experiences.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure for a digital art collection showing how to define traits with trait_type and value pairs

useCases: Create unique digital art collections with distinct characteristics
Define rarity traits for collectible NFTs
Specify visual attributes like fur color and accessories for animated NFTs
Structure metadata for NFT marketplaces
Display NFT characteristics using Certificate of Trust
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations Next Blockchain Badges Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful?

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive API reference for NFT.zK operations including creating, listing, transferring, and sending NFTs by email. Covers asynchronous operations with jobId tracking, method parameters, request/response examples, and NFT metadata management. Details transfer codes for email-based ownership transfers.

codeExamples.description: Create a new NFT on the Mumbai testnet with name, description, image URL, and rarity attributes
Response from NFT creation API call returning a jobId for tracking the asynchronous blockchain transaction
Completed NFT creation response containing transaction hash, IPFS hashes, URLs, and transfer codes for ownership management

useCases: Mint new NFTs on blockchain networks with custom metadata
List and manage NFT inventory with timestamp-based filtering
Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallet addresses
Send NFTs to users via email without requiring a Web3 wallet
Track NFT creation status using asynchronous job tracking

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Call the API with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL/IPFS URI, and optional attributes. Receive a jobId for tracking.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the returned jobId to check the blockchain transaction status and retrieve the completed NFT details including transaction hash and IPFS hashes.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Use the 'transfer' operation with nftId and wallet address, or 'send' operation with nftId and email address to transfer NFT ownership. Email transfers generate owner and receiver codes.

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations Create NFT List NFTs Transfer NFT Send NFT by Email NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Operations BWS.NFT.zK API Operations. Create NFT This operation is asynchronous (the blockchain network takes a while to confirm transactions). Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. Create NFT Request Example cURL Create NFT Fetch Response Create NFT Fetch Response Example The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) NFT Urls Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL Transfer Codes Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. List NFTs Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. List NFT Method Parameters If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. List IPFS Files call Response LIST NFT Request Example cURL List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. List NFT Fetch Response Example Transfer NFT This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Transfer NFT Request Example cURL Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example Send NFT by Email Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Send NFT Request Example cURL Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example Previous Solution Overview Next NFT Attributes (traits) Last updated 13 days ago Was this helpful? Create NFT Creates a new NFT. pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of NFT.zK solution covering NFT ownership models, transfer mechanisms, and data storage architecture. Explains wallet-based and email-based ownership transfers, IPFS hosting for images and metadata, and available blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum with their smart contract addresses.

codeExamples.description: Example API call JSON showing how to specify an NFT image using a web URL that will be automatically uploaded to IPFS
Example NFT metadata structure showing image referenced via IPFS URI, demonstrating decentralized storage format

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to users without Web3 wallets using email addresses
Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallet addresses directly
Custody NFTs linked to BWS accounts before final transfer
Host NFT images and metadata on IPFS for decentralized storage
Deploy NFTs on multiple blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with Image
implementationSteps.description: Provide NFT image as either a web URL or IPFS URI. The system automatically uploads web URLs to IPFS and stores metadata on IPFS as well.

implementationSteps.title: Initial Custody
implementationSteps.description: Newly created NFT is initially linked to your BWS account for custody before ownership transfer. You maintain control until you choose to transfer.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Choose between transferring to a blockchain wallet address using the Transfer NFT operation, or to an email address for users without wallets. Email recipients receive notifications and can later transfer to their own wallets.

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview NFT Ownership NFT Data Location Available Networks Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Easily create NFTs. NFT Ownership When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options: Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address. Transfer ownership to an email address. Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account. Transfer NFT Ownership to a Wallet If you want to transfer an NFT ownership to another wallet address, you can simply use the Transfer NFT operation indicating the new blockchain wallet address owner. Transfer NFT Ownership to an Email Address If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address. Email received for the new NFT owner to hold and get ownership We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to. Transfer ownership page for wasy NFT transfers Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address. NFT Metadata and Image Location NFT Image Your NFT image is always hosted in IPFS. To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as: a web addressable URL, or, as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki). API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image. In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required. NFT Metadata Your NFT metadata is always hosted in IPFS. When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS, NFT Metadata Example Your NFT blockchain transaction then points to IPFS, as you can see on the following example: NFT Creation Transaction Parameters Networks We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. We currently offer NFT creation in Polygon and Ethereum blockchains. Blockchain Network Id Description Matchain matchain Matchain blockchain. Polygon polygon Polygon blockchain. mumbai Polygon test network. Ethereum ethereum Ethereum blockchain We recommend using the Polygon Mumbai network for tests. Smart Contracts We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. Click on Contract Address to check the verified contract. paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
